signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.72; 160.00 is the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — 157.00 is the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: HV30 at 8.2%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 9.4% — options pricing a 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.14: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. CFTC LF net +41,675 (week Apr 14), WoW delta −1,101 for second consecutive week; AM not reducing. AUD/USD at 0.713; retail 89% short — institutional vs retail divergence at extreme. Late-trend caution; retail capitulation risk if price extends higher.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry neutral; GBP/USD at 1.3465. GBP LF net long +28,426; AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. ATM IV 9.5% near HV; 25d RR flat — options neutral. GBP/USD mid-range; 1.3500 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT + OIS
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 (specs short) vs RBNZ OIS hike 100% priced. Specs are fading a hawkish CB. If RBNZ delivers or signals hike, short squeeze risk is elevated. NZD/USD at 0.5855; 0.5900 is the next level on a potential squeeze.
